reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. An attacker can trigger this via P2P by sending valid DataColumnSidecars to any Prysm node. The ReconstructDataColumnSidecars function at reconstruction.go:79-82 iterates sidecarByIndex map (created at line 54) without sorting, building cellsIndices in randomized order due to Go's randomized map iteration. This unsorted slice is passed to kzg.RecoverCellsAndKZGProofs at line 86, which calls the c-kzg-4844 library that requires sorted indices (verified by test at fulu__kzg__recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs_test.go:45-57 which expects failure for unsorted input). The function will non-deterministically fail, breaking PeerDAS reconstruction. The 01e property from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json (lines 150-165, PROP-57888860-inv-006) correctly states: 'Recovery cell_indices must be unique and sorted in ascending order' with assertion 'forall recover(cell_indices): is_sorted(cell_indices) AND is_unique(cell_indices)'. Severity: Medium justified by impact on >5% of network (Prysm ~31% CL share), P2P attack vector, but non-deterministic failure reduces from High to Medium.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Recovery cell_indices must be unique and sorted in ascending order. (01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json line 150)
